name: Almond Popovers
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 425 degrees. Grease popover pan or 6 custard cups. Blend together eggs, milk, oil, and salt. Blend in flour until smooth; beat at least 1 minute on an electric mixer or 3 minutes with a rotary beater. Fill popover pan or custard cups half full. Sprinkle almonds over tops. Bake 40-45 minutes or until brown and firm to touch. For crisper popovers, prick sides of each with a fork, reduce oven temperature to 350 degrees, and bake for an additional 20 minutes. Yield: 6 large or 11 small.
Here are some variations from Southern Living - Our Best Recipes (1973 edition):
- Bacon Popovers: Cook 3 strips of bacon until crisp. Drain. Substitute 1 tablespoon bacon drippings for oil in the recipe and finely crumbled bacon for almonds. To serve, fill with scrambled eggs.
- Pecan Popovers: Substitute 2 tablespoons finely chopped pecans for almonds. To serve, fill baked popovers with creamed chicken.
- Parsley Popovers: Blend 1 tablespoon dried parsley into the egg mixture and omit almonds. To serve, fill baked popovers with shrimp salad.
- Chili Popovers: Blend 2 teaspoons chili powder into the egg mixture and omit almonds. To serve, fill baked popovers with chili con carne.
- Cheese Popovers: Blend 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese or shredded American cheese into the egg mixture and omit almonds. To serve, fill baked popovers with apple sauce or baked apple slices.
Hint: Preheat your pans for a crisper crust. Baking time remains the same.
